requires.rationale: URN or globally-unique ID is first-class identity

structure.description: Canonical ID (URN, DOI, ARN) shown next to entity title with one-click copy button. Often muted styling, monospace font. Target: cross-platform reference, API calls, agent-tool inputs.

rationale.hypothesis: When entities are referenced across systems (CI, agents, API), the canonical ID is the actual cross-system handle. Hiding it forces the user to inspect URLs or read API docs. Surfacing + copy makes integration trivial.

evidence.description: URN visible and copyable on entity page
ARN copy button on every resource

counterexample.description: If never referenced externally, canonical ID adds clutter

shouldMatch.reason: Workflow ID used in CI tools
ISIN as canonical cross-platform asset id
